analysis_conclusion: Gemini, GPT OSS, Mistral, Qwen, and GLM all returned status=ok and schema_valid=true.
The public graph exposes 1 primary candidate, 5 validation target(s), and 2 monitor-only item(s) after the analysis-window boundary is applied.

db_corpus_coverage.prompt_boundary_policy: Sanitized DB rows are assigned to Evidence Items and review chunks; raw rows are not copied directly into provider prompts.

log_observations: The run used 23,397/23,397 sanitized amazon-notify DB rows from 2026-06-09T18:35:25Z to 2026-06-16T18:35:25Z as the coverage corpus; direct raw-row prompt count was 0.
The local-first Evidence Bundle retained 3,821 grouped evidence items, including 3,400 singleton pattern(s), 3,630 low-frequency pattern(s), and 0 deterministic signals.

The single-prompt projection used 140 selected evidence items covering 19,268 occurrences (82.3% of the sanitized corpus); provider synthesis covered 3,821/3,821 grouped Evidence Items via chunked calls.

Single-prompt projection coverage is occurrence-weighted, not raw-row coverage: the full sanitized amazon-notify bundle keeps 23,397 rows and 3,821 grouped Evidence Items, while the bounded projection tracks 140 high-signal Evidence Items representing 19,268 repeated occurrences. Multi-provider synthesis then covered 3,821/3,821 grouped Evidence Items through chunked provider calls across up to 110 chunk(s), so low-frequency Evidence Items are analyzed instead of being dropped by frequency.
Coverage classes are preserved as review boundaries: pattern, rare, singleton, and state_transition.
analysis_context.model_projection_interpretation: Single-prompt projection coverage is occurrence-weighted, not raw-row coverage: the full sanitized amazon-notify bundle keeps 23,397 rows and 3,821 grouped Evidence Items, while the bounded projection tracks 140 high-signal Evidence Items representing 19,268 repeated occurrences. Multi-provider synthesis then covered 3,821/3,821 grouped Evidence Items through chunked provider calls across up to 110 chunk(s), so low-frequency Evidence Items are analyzed instead of being dropped by frequency.
analysis_context.model_projection_policy: Every sanitized DB row in the analysis window is assigned to a coverage ledger entry before provider prompts. Provider prompts use chunked Evidence Corpora; prompt-excluded row-level evidence remains visible through coverage accounting and chunk manifests.

provider_explanations.claim_text: Downstream dependency health (e.g., Gmail watch renewal) has not been verified.

provider_explanations.operational_mechanism: If the Gmail watch expires or returns errors, amazon-notify would have no events to process.
provider_explanations.suspected_issue: External API failures could prevent notifications despite the service appearing healthy.
provider_explanations.why_it_matters: Dependency outages directly impact the primary user outcome of delivering notifications.
provider_explanations.why_not_promoted: No logs or metrics about downstream API success/failure are present.

provider_explanations.claim_text: A downstream dependency (e.g., Gmail watch renewal, Amazon notification API, or Discord webhook) may be failing, preventing notifications from being delivered despite successful internal runs.
provider_explanations.next_validation_question: Do the external API logs (Gmail, Amazon, Discord) show any errors or rejected requests during the incident window?
provider_explanations.operational_mechanism: The amazon-notify service invokes external APIs after each successful run; if those APIs reject requests or are unavailable, no notifications are sent.
provider_explanations.suspected_issue: Downstream service outage or authentication failure.
provider_explanations.why_it_matters: User‑facing notifications would stop, violating the critical user outcome of continuous notification delivery.
provider_explanations.why_not_promoted: No direct evidence of downstream failures is present; the logs only show internal success.
